title: Katy Perry

infobox.value: Katheryn Elizabeth Hudson   (1984-10-25) October 25, 1984 (age 41)  [Santa Barbara, California](/wiki/Santa_Barbara,_California "Santa Barbara, California"), U.S.

* [Songs](/wiki/List_of_songs_recorded_by_Katy_Perry "List of songs recorded by Katy Perry") * [discography](/wiki/Katy_Perry_discography "Katy Perry discography") * [performances](/wiki/List_of_Katy_Perry_live_performances "List of Katy Perry live performances") * [videography](/wiki/Katy_Perry_videography "Katy Perry videography")
[Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Katy_Perry "List of awards and nominations received by Katy Perry")

(born October 25, 1984), known professionally as **Katy Perry**, is an American singer, songwriter, and television personality. Known for her influence on [pop music](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") and her [camp](/wiki/Camp_(style) "Camp (style)") style, she has been dubbed the "[Queen of Camp](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")" by [*Vogue*](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)") and *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American singer (born 1984)

This article is about the singer. For the Israeli civil servant, see [Katy Perry (prison officer)](/wiki/Katy_Perry_(prison_officer) "Katy Perry (prison officer)"). For the Australian fashion designer, see [Katie Perry](/wiki/Katie_Perry "Katie Perry").

"Katy Hudson" redirects here. For the album, see [*Katy Hudson* (album)](/wiki/Katy_Hudson_(album) "Katy Hudson (album)").

**Katheryn Elizabeth Hudson** (born October 25, 1984), known professionally as **Katy Perry**, is an American singer, songwriter, and television personality. Known for her influence on [pop music](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") and her [camp](/wiki/Camp_(style) "Camp (style)") style, she has been dubbed the "[Queen of Camp](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music "Honorific nicknames in popular music")" by [*Vogue*](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)") and *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*.

At 16, Perry released the unsuccessful [gospel](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music") album *[Katy Hudson](/wiki/Katy_Hudson_(album) "Katy Hudson (album)")* (2001) under [Red Hill Records](/wiki/Pamplin_Music "Pamplin Music"). After moving to Los Angeles at 17, she signed to [Capitol Records](/wiki/Capitol_Records "Capitol Records") and rose to fame with the [pop rock](/wiki/Pop_rock "Pop rock") album *[One of the Boys](/wiki/One_of_the_Boys "One of the Boys")* (2008). Its lead single, "[I Kissed a Girl](/wiki/I_Kissed_a_Girl "I Kissed a Girl")", topped the U.S. [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). Perry's [disco](/wiki/Disco "Disco")-influenced pop record *[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream "Teenage Dream")* (2010) became the only album by a woman to spawn five U.S. number-one singles: "[California Gurls](/wiki/California_Gurls "California Gurls")", "[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream_(Katy_Perry_song) "Teenage Dream (Katy Perry song)")", "[Firework](/wiki/Firework_(song) "Firework (song)")", "[E.T.](/wiki/E.T._(song) "E.T. (song)")", and "[Last Friday Night (T.G.I.F.)](/wiki/Last_Friday_Night_(T.G.I.F.) "Last Friday Night (T.G.I.F.)")". Its reissue, subtitled *[The Complete Confection](/wiki/Teenage_Dream:_The_Complete_Confection "Teenage Dream: The Complete Confection")* (2012), produced the U.S. number-one single "[Part of Me](/wiki/Part_of_Me_(Katy_Perry_song) "Part of Me (Katy Perry song)")". The [dance](/wiki/Dance_music "Dance music")-inspired *[Prism](/wiki/Prism_(Katy_Perry_album) "Prism (Katy Perry album)")* (2013) yielded two U.S. number-one singles, "[Roar](/wiki/Roar_(song) "Roar (song)")" and "[Dark Horse](/wiki/Dark_Horse_(Katy_Perry_song) "Dark Horse (Katy Perry song)")". Perry's subsequent albums, *[Witness](/wiki/Witness_(Katy_Perry_album) "Witness (Katy Perry album)")* (2017), *[Smile](/wiki/Smile_(Katy_Perry_album) "Smile (Katy Perry album)")* (2020) and *[143](/wiki/143_(Katy_Perry_album) "143 (Katy Perry album)")* (2024), were met with varied critical and commmercial success.

Perry is one of the [best-selling music artists of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists"), with estimated sales of over 151 million records worldwide. Six of her songs have received Diamond certifications from the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA). The [world's highest-paid female musician](/wiki/Forbes_list_of_the_world%27s_highest-paid_musicians#Female "Forbes list of the world's highest-paid musicians") in 2015 and 2018, she was named by [*Billboard*](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)") as one of the [greatest pop stars of the 21st century](/wiki/Billboard%27s_Greatest_Pop_Stars#21st_century "Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars"). [Her accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Katy_Perry "List of awards and nominations received by Katy Perry") include 20 *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")*, five [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award"), five [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards "American Music Awards") (including the [Special Achievement Award](/wiki/American_Music_Award_for_Achievement "American Music Award for Achievement")), a [Brit Award](/wiki/Brit_Award "Brit Award"), a [Juno Award](/wiki/Juno_Award "Juno Award"), and seven [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards") (including the [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award")). Perry was the first artist to have multiple videos reach one billion views on [Vevo](/wiki/Vevo "Vevo") and [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube"), with nine videos in total. With an estimated net worth of $360 million, she is [among the world's wealthiest musicians](/wiki/List_of_music_artists_by_net_worth "List of music artists by net worth").

Outside of music, Perry released an autobiographical documentary titled *[Katy Perry: Part of Me](/wiki/Katy_Perry:_Part_of_Me "Katy Perry: Part of Me")* in 2012, voiced [Smurfette](/wiki/Smurfette "Smurfette") in [*The Smurfs* film series](/wiki/The_Smurfs_in_film#Sony_Pictures_Animation_films_(2011–2017) "The Smurfs in film") (2011–2013) and launched her own shoe line [Katy Perry Collections](/wiki/Katy_Perry_Collections "Katy Perry Collections") in 2017. She served as a judge on *[American Idol](/wiki/American_Idol "American Idol")* from the [sixteenth season](/wiki/American_Idol_season_16 "American Idol season 16") in 2018 to the [twenty-second season](/wiki/American_Idol_season_22 "American Idol season 22") in 2024. In 2025, Perry flew into space aboard [Blue Origin NS-31](/wiki/Blue_Origin_NS-31 "Blue Origin NS-31"), the first all-female space flight since 1963, which received significant media coverage. She is an advocate for [LGBT rights](/wiki/LGBT_rights "LGBT rights") and [women's rights](/wiki/Women%27s_rights "Women's rights"), and was named a [UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador](/wiki/List_of_UNICEF_Goodwill_Ambassadors "List of UNICEF Goodwill Ambassadors") in 2013. Her philanthropy and activism focus on children's education and [HIV/AIDS](/wiki/HIV/AIDS "HIV/AIDS") awareness.

## Life and career

### 1984–1999: Early life and family

Katheryn Elizabeth Hudson was born on October 25, 1984, in [Santa Barbara, California](/wiki/Santa_Barbara,_California "Santa Barbara, California"), to [Pentecostal](/wiki/Pentecostalism "Pentecostalism") pastors Mary Christine (née Perry) and Maurice Keith Hudson.[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201205:23-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Friedlander_2012_15-2) Both of her parents turned to religion after a "wild youth".[[3]](#cite_note-GraffBold-3) Perry has English, German, Irish, and Portuguese ancestry.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Through her mother, she is a niece of film director [Frank Perry](/wiki/Frank_Perry "Frank Perry").[[5]](#cite_note-5) She has a younger brother named David, who is also a singer,[[6]](#cite_note-6) and an older sister, Angela.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

From ages three to 11, Perry frequently moved across the country as her very strict parents set up churches before settling again in Santa Barbara. Growing up, she attended religious schools and camps, including Paradise Valley Christian School in [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona") and Santa Barbara Christian School in California during her [elementary years](/wiki/Primary_school "Primary school").[[2]](#cite_note-Friedlander_2012_15-2)[[8]](#cite_note-8) The family struggled financially,[[9]](#cite_note-GQ-9) sometimes using [food stamps](/wiki/Supplemental_Nutrition_Assistance_Program "Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program") and eating food from the food bank which also fed the congregation at her parents' church.[[10]](#cite_note-RS2010-10)

Growing up, Perry and her siblings were not allowed to eat the cereal [Lucky Charms](/wiki/Lucky_Charms "Lucky Charms") as the word "luck" reminded their mother of [Lucifer](/wiki/Lucifer "Lucifer"), and were also required to call [deviled eggs](/wiki/Deviled_egg "Deviled egg") "angeled eggs".[[11]](#cite_note-11) Perry primarily listened to [gospel music](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music"),[[12]](#cite_note-MontgomeryGospel-12) as secular music was generally discouraged in the family's home. She discovered popular music through CDs she smuggled home from her friends.[[13]](#cite_note-Childhood-13) Perry later recalled a story about how a friend of hers played "[You Oughta Know](/wiki/You_Oughta_Know "You Oughta Know")" by [Alanis Morissette](/wiki/Alanis_Morissette "Alanis Morissette"), which influenced her songwriting and singing.[[14]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201222:22-14)

While not strictly identifying as religious, she has stated, "I pray all the time – for self-control, for humility."[[15]](#cite_note-Marie-15) Wanting to be like her sister Angela, Perry began singing by practicing with her sister's cassette tapes. She performed the tracks in front of her parents, who let her take vocal lessons like Angela was doing at the time. She began [training](/wiki/Vocal_pedagogy "Vocal pedagogy") at age nine;[[16]](#cite_note-16) she was also incorporated into her parents' ministry,[[3]](#cite_note-GraffBold-3) singing in church from ages nine to 17.[[17]](#cite_note-17) At 13, Perry was given her first guitar for her birthday,[[3]](#cite_note-GraffBold-3)[[18]](#cite_note-18) and publicly performed songs she wrote.[[9]](#cite_note-GQ-9)

She tried to "be a bit like the typical Californian girl" while growing up, and started rollerskating, skateboarding, and surfing as a teenager. Her brother David described her as a "[tomboy](/wiki/Tomboy "Tomboy")" during her adolescence, which Perry talks about on her song "One of the Boys".[[19]](#cite_note-19) She took dancing lessons and learned how to [swing](/wiki/Swing_(dance) "Swing (dance)"), [Lindy Hop](/wiki/Lindy_Hop "Lindy Hop"), and [jitterbug](/wiki/Jitterbug "Jitterbug").[[20]](#cite_note-20) Perry completed her [General Educational Development](/wiki/General_Educational_Development "General Educational Development") (GED) requirements early at age 15,[[21]](#cite_note-21) during her first year of high school,[[22]](#cite_note-Elle-22) and left [Dos Pueblos High School](/wiki/Dos_Pueblos_High_School "Dos Pueblos High School") to pursue a music career.[[23]](#cite_note-Hudson37-23)

### 2000–2006: Career beginnings, *Katy Hudson*, and *Fingerprints*

Perry briefly had vocal lessons with a woman named Agatha Danoff[[24]](#cite_note-24) in facilities rented from the [Music Academy of the West](/wiki/Music_Academy_of_the_West "Music Academy of the West").[[25]](#cite_note-25) Her singing caught the attention of rock artists Steve Thomas and [Jennifer Knapp](/wiki/Jennifer_Knapp "Jennifer Knapp") from [Nashville, Tennessee](/wiki/Nashville,_Tennessee "Nashville, Tennessee"), who brought her there to improve her writing skills.[[23]](#cite_note-Hudson37-23) In Nashville, she started recording [demos](/wiki/Demo_(music) "Demo (music)") and learned how to write songs and play guitar.[[12]](#cite_note-MontgomeryGospel-12) Perry signed with [Red Hill Records](/wiki/Pamplin_Music "Pamplin Music") and recorded her debut album, a [contemporary Christian](/wiki/Contemporary_Christian_Music "Contemporary Christian Music") record titled *[Katy Hudson](/wiki/Katy_Hudson_(album) "Katy Hudson (album)")*, which was released on March 6, 2001.[[26]](#cite_note-26) She also joined [Earthsuit](/wiki/Earthsuit "Earthsuit") and [V\*Enna](/wiki/V*Enna "V*Enna") that year to perform as part of [Phil Joel](/wiki/Phil_Joel "Phil Joel")'s Strangely Normal Tour[[27]](#cite_note-27)[[28]](#cite_note-28) and embarked on other performances of her own in the United States.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

*Katy Hudson* received mixed reviews from critics and was commercially unsuccessful, selling an estimated 200 copies before the label ceased operations in December.[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31) Transitioning from gospel music to secular music, Perry started working with producer [Glen Ballard](/wiki/Glen_Ballard "Glen Ballard"),[[32]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201221:11-32)[[33]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201228:19-33) and moved to Los Angeles at the age of 17.[[34]](#cite_note-WMag-34) She opted to work with Ballard due to his past work with Alanis Morissette, one of her major inspirations. In 2003, she briefly performed as Katheryn Perry, to avoid confusion with actress [Kate Hudson](/wiki/Kate_Hudson "Kate Hudson"), and later adopted the stage name "Katy Perry", using her mother's maiden name.[[35]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201238:33-35) In 2010, she recalled that "[Thinking of You](/wiki/Thinking_of_You_(Katy_Perry_song) "Thinking of You (Katy Perry song)")" was one of the first songs she wrote after moving to Los Angeles.[[36]](#cite_note-36) Perry would also perform at the [Hotel Café](/wiki/Hotel_Caf%C3%A9 "Hotel Café"), performing new music while she was between record labels.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

In 2004, she signed to Ballard's label, Java Records, which was then affiliated with [The Island Def Jam Music Group](/wiki/The_Island_Def_Jam_Music_Group "The Island Def Jam Music Group"). Perry began work on a solo record due for release in March 2005, but the record was shelved after Java was dropped.[[38]](#cite_note-glen_ballard_billboard-38) Ballard then introduced her to [Tim Devine](/wiki/Tim_Devine "Tim Devine"), an [A&R](/wiki/Artists_and_repertoire "Artists and repertoire") executive at [Columbia Records](/wiki/Columbia_Records "Columbia Records"), and she was signed as a solo artist. By November 2006, Perry had finished writing and recording material for her Columbia debut titled *Fingerprints* (with some of the material from this time appearing on *One of the Boys*) which was planned for release in 2007.[[39]](#cite_note-39) Some of the material from *Fingerprints* that did not make it on *One of the Boys* was given to other artists, such as "[I Do Not Hook Up](/wiki/I_Do_Not_Hook_Up "I Do Not Hook Up")" and "[Long Shot](/wiki/Long_Shot_(Kelly_Clarkson_song) "Long Shot (Kelly Clarkson song)")" to [Kelly Clarkson](/wiki/Kelly_Clarkson "Kelly Clarkson"), and "[Rock God](/wiki/Rock_God "Rock God")" to [Selena Gomez & the Scene](/wiki/Selena_Gomez_%26_the_Scene "Selena Gomez & the Scene").[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)

Perry worked with songwriters including [Desmond Child](/wiki/Desmond_Child "Desmond Child"), [Greg Wells](/wiki/Greg_Wells "Greg Wells"), [Butch Walker](/wiki/Butch_Walker "Butch Walker"), [Scott Cutler](/wiki/Scott_Cutler "Scott Cutler"), [Anne Preven](/wiki/Anne_Preven "Anne Preven"), [the Matrix](/wiki/The_Matrix_(production_team) "The Matrix (production team)"), [Kara DioGuardi](/wiki/Kara_DioGuardi "Kara DioGuardi"), [Max Martin](/wiki/Max_Martin "Max Martin"), and [Dr. Luke](/wiki/Dr._Luke "Dr. Luke").[[42]](#cite_note-Anokute_Hit_Quarters-42)[[43]](#cite_note-billboard_long-43) In addition, after Devine suggested that songwriting team the Matrix become a "real group", she recorded an album, *[The Matrix](/wiki/The_Matrix_(album) "The Matrix (album)")*, with them.[[44]](#cite_note-44) This featured her and Adam Longlands as lead vocalists.[[45]](#cite_note-45) *The Matrix* was planned for release in 2004 but was shelved due to creative differences. Perry was dropped from Columbia in 2006 as *Fingerprints* neared completion. After the label dropped her, she worked at an independent [A&R](/wiki/A%26R "A&R") company, Taxi Music.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

Perry had minor success prior to her breakthrough. One of the songs she had recorded for her album with Ballard, "Simple", was featured on the soundtrack to the 2005 film *[The Sisterhood of the Traveling Pants](/wiki/The_Sisterhood_of_the_Traveling_Pants_(film) "The Sisterhood of the Traveling Pants (film)")*.[[47]](#cite_note-Summers-47) Perry provided backing vocals on [Mick Jagger](/wiki/Mick_Jagger "Mick Jagger")'s song "[Old Habits Die Hard](/wiki/Old_Habits_Die_Hard "Old Habits Die Hard")",[[48]](#cite_note-48) which was included on [the soundtrack](/wiki/Alfie_(2004_film_soundtrack) "Alfie (2004 film soundtrack)") to the 2004 film *[Alfie](/wiki/Alfie_(2004_film) "Alfie (2004 film)")*.[[49]](#cite_note-allmusicbio-49) In September 2004, *[Blender](/wiki/Blender_(magazine) "Blender (magazine)")* named her "The Next Big Thing".[[47]](#cite_note-Summers-47) She recorded background vocals on [P.O.D.](/wiki/P.O.D. "P.O.D.")'s single "[Goodbye for Now](/wiki/Goodbye_for_Now_(song) "Goodbye for Now (song)")", was featured at the end of its music video in 2006, and performed it with them on *[The Tonight Show with Jay Leno](/wiki/The_Tonight_Show_with_Jay_Leno "The Tonight Show with Jay Leno")*.[[50]](#cite_note-50) That year, Perry also appeared in the music video for "Learn to Fly" by [Carbon Leaf](/wiki/Carbon_Leaf "Carbon Leaf"), and she played the love interest of her then-boyfriend, [Gym Class Heroes](/wiki/Gym_Class_Heroes "Gym Class Heroes") lead singer [Travie McCoy](/wiki/Travie_McCoy "Travie McCoy"), in the band's music video for "[Cupid's Chokehold](/wiki/Cupid%27s_Chokehold "Cupid's Chokehold")".[[51]](#cite_note-51)

### 2007–2009: Breakthrough with *One of the Boys*

After Columbia dropped Perry, [Angelica Cob-Baehler](/wiki/Angelica_Cob-Baehler "Angelica Cob-Baehler"), then a publicity executive at the label, brought Perry's demos to [Virgin Records](/wiki/Virgin_Records "Virgin Records") chairman [Jason Flom](/wiki/Jason_Flom "Jason Flom"). Flom was convinced that she could be a breakthrough star and she was signed to [Capitol Records](/wiki/Capitol_Records "Capitol Records") in April 2007. The label arranged for her to work with Dr. Luke to add an "undeniable smash" to her existing material.[[52]](#cite_note-hitquarters-52)[[53]](#cite_note-53) Perry and Dr. Luke co-wrote the songs "[I Kissed a Girl](/wiki/I_Kissed_a_Girl "I Kissed a Girl")" and "[Hot n Cold](/wiki/Hot_n_Cold "Hot n Cold")" for her second album *[One of the Boys](/wiki/One_of_the_Boys "One of the Boys")*. A campaign was started with the November 2007 release of the video to "[Ur So Gay](/wiki/Ur_So_Gay "Ur So Gay")", a song aimed at introducing her to the music market.[[54]](#cite_note-54) A [digital EP of the same name](/wiki/Ur_So_Gay_(EP) "Ur So Gay (EP)") was also released that month.[[55]](#cite_note-55) [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna") helped publicize the song by praising it on the *JohnJay & Rich* radio show in April 2008,[[56]](#cite_note-56) stating "Ur So Gay" was her "favorite song" at the time.[[57]](#cite_note-57) In March 2008, Perry made a cameo appearance as a club singer in the *[Wildfire](/wiki/Wildfire_(2005_TV_series) "Wildfire (2005 TV series)")* episode "Life's Too Short"[[58]](#cite_note-58) and appeared as herself during a photo shoot that June on *[The Young and the Restless](/wiki/The_Young_and_the_Restless "The Young and the Restless")* for the show's magazine *Restless Style*.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

[![Katy Perry performing on the Warped Tour 2008](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/Katy_Perry_performing.jpg/250px-Katy_Perry_performing.jpg)](/wiki/File:Katy_Perry_performing.jpg)

Perry performing at [Cricket Wireless Amphitheatre](/wiki/North_Island_Credit_Union_Amphitheatre "North Island Credit Union Amphitheatre") during the [Warped Tour](/wiki/Warped_Tour "Warped Tour") in August 2008

Perry released her first single with Capitol, "I Kissed a Girl", on April 28, 2008, as the lead single from *One of the Boys*.[[60]](#cite_note-60) The first station to pick up the song was [WRVW](/wiki/WRVW "WRVW") in Nashville, who were inundated with enthusiastic calls the first three days they played it.[[61]](#cite_note-hitquarters.com2-61) The track topped the U.S. [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") on July 5, 2008, and remained number one for seven consecutive weeks.[[62]](#cite_note-62) "I Kissed a Girl" created controversy among both religious and [LGBT](/wiki/LGBT "LGBT") groups. The former criticized its homosexual theme, while the latter accused her of using [bi-curiosity](/wiki/Bi-curiosity "Bi-curiosity") to sell records. In response to speculation that her parents opposed her music and career, Perry told [MTV News](/wiki/MTV_News "MTV News") that they had no problems with her success.[[63]](#cite_note-VenaRumors2-63)

*One of the Boys*, released on June 17, 2008, garnered mixed critical reviews and reached number nine on the U.S. [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200").[[64]](#cite_note-64)[[65]](#cite_note-65) The album went on to sell seven million copies worldwide.[[66]](#cite_note-66) "Hot n Cold" was released the following September and became the album's second successful single,[[67]](#cite_note-67) reaching number three on the *Billboard* Hot 100,[[68]](#cite_note-Hot100History2-68) while topping charts in Germany,[[69]](#cite_note-69) Canada,[[70]](#cite_note-CanadaCharts2-70) the Netherlands, and Austria.[[71]](#cite_note-71)[[72]](#cite_note-72) Later singles "Thinking of You" and "[Waking Up in Vegas](/wiki/Waking_Up_in_Vegas "Waking Up in Vegas")" were released in 2009 and reached the top 30 of the Hot 100.[[68]](#cite_note-Hot100History2-68)[[73]](#cite_note-73)[[74]](#cite_note-74)

From June to August 2008, Perry traveled with McCoy on the [Warped Tour](/wiki/Warped_Tour "Warped Tour").[[75]](#cite_note-75)[[76]](#cite_note-76) As a symbol of their commitment to one another,[[77]](#cite_note-77) he gave her a diamond [promise ring](/wiki/Pre-engagement_ring "Pre-engagement ring") before the tour,[[78]](#cite_note-78)[[79]](#cite_note-79) and he wore a ring with "Katy" inscribed on it.[[80]](#cite_note-:10-80) That September, a limited-edition Katy Perry doll was produced by [Integrity Toys](/wiki/Integrity_Toys "Integrity Toys") in response to her growing popularity.[[81]](#cite_note-81)[[82]](#cite_note-82) She hosted the [2008 MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/2008_MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "2008 MTV Europe Music Awards") in November and won the award for [Best New Act](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Award_for_Best_New_Act "MTV Europe Music Award for Best New Act").[[83]](#cite_note-83) Following their breakup in December 2008,[[84]](#cite_note-TravieSplit2-84)[[85]](#cite_note-85) Perry and McCoy reconciled in April 2009[[86]](#cite_note-86)[[87]](#cite_note-87) before she ended their relationship again later that year.[[88]](#cite_note-88)

The Matrix's self-titled album, which Perry had recorded with the band in 2004, was released on [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") on January 27, 2009, as a result of her solo success.[[89]](#cite_note-allmusicbio2-89)[[90]](#cite_note-KaufmanMatrix2-90) At the [2009 Brit Awards](/wiki/2009_Brit_Awards "2009 Brit Awards") the next month, she won the award for [International Female Solo Artist](/wiki/Brit_Award_for_International_Female_Solo_Artist "Brit Award for International Female Solo Artist").[[91]](#cite_note-Brit20092-91) Perry embarked on her first headlining world tour, the [Hello Katy Tour](/wiki/Hello_Katy_Tour "Hello Katy Tour"), from January to November 2009 to support *One of the Boys*.[[92]](#cite_note-Hudson832-92) On August 4, 2009, she performed as opening act for one date of [No Doubt](/wiki/No_Doubt "No Doubt")'s [2009 Summer Tour](/wiki/2009_Summer_Tour "2009 Summer Tour").[[93]](#cite_note-93) Perry also hosted the [2009 MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/2009_MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "2009 MTV Europe Music Awards") that November, becoming the first person to host two consecutive ceremonies of the European awards.[[94]](#cite_note-94)

On July 22, 2009, Perry recorded a live album titled *[MTV Unplugged](/wiki/MTV_Unplugged_(Katy_Perry_EP) "MTV Unplugged (Katy Perry EP)")*, which featured [acoustic](/wiki/Acoustic_music "Acoustic music") performances of five tracks from *One of the Boys* as well as one new song, "Brick by Brick", and a cover of [Fountains of Wayne](/wiki/Fountains_of_Wayne "Fountains of Wayne")'s "Hackensack".[[95]](#cite_note-UnpluggedNotes-95) It was released on November 17, 2009.[[96]](#cite_note-MTV_Unplugged_Album-96) Perry also appeared on two singles with other artists; she was featured on a remix of [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado")-based band [3OH!3](/wiki/3OH!3 "3OH!3")'s song "[Starstrukk](/wiki/Starstrukk "Starstrukk")" in September 2009,[[97]](#cite_note-97) and on a duet with [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland") entitled "[If We Ever Meet Again](/wiki/If_We_Ever_Meet_Again "If We Ever Meet Again")", from his album *[Shock Value II](/wiki/Shock_Value_II "Shock Value II")*, three months later.[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99) The *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* recognized her in its 2010 edition as the "Best Start on the U.S. Digital Chart by a Female Artist", for digital single sales of over two million copies.[[100]](#cite_note-Guinness2010-100)

Perry met her future husband [Russell Brand](/wiki/Russell_Brand "Russell Brand") in mid-2009 while filming a cameo appearance for his film *[Get Him to the Greek](/wiki/Get_Him_to_the_Greek "Get Him to the Greek")*. Her scene, in which the two kiss, does not appear in the film.[[101]](#cite_note-GreekExplain-101) She began dating Brand after meeting him again that September at the [2009 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2009_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2009 MTV Video Music Awards").[[102]](#cite_note-RussellDating-102) The couple became engaged on December 31, 2009, while vacationing in [Rajasthan](/wiki/Rajasthan "Rajasthan"), India.[[103]](#cite_note-Engaged-103)

### 2010–2012: *Teenage Dream* and marriage

After serving as a guest judge on *[American Idol](/wiki/American_Idol "American Idol")*,[[104]](#cite_note-104) Perry released "[California Gurls](/wiki/California_Gurls "California Gurls")" featuring [Snoop Dogg](/wiki/Snoop_Dogg "Snoop Dogg") on May 7, 2010.[[105]](#cite_note-105) The song was the lead single from her third studio album, *[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream "Teenage Dream")*, and reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100 in June.[[106]](#cite_note-106) She also served as a guest judge on [British *The X Factor*](/wiki/The_X_Factor_(British_TV_series) "The X Factor (British TV series)") later that month[[107]](#cite_note-XFactorJudge-107) before releasing the album's second single, "[Teenage Dream](/wiki/Teenage_Dream_(Katy_Perry_song) "Teenage Dream (Katy Perry song)")", in July.[[108]](#cite_note-108) "Teenage Dream" reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100 in September.[[109]](#cite_note-109) Released on August 24, 2010,[[110]](#cite_note-110) *Teenage Dream* debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200,[[111]](#cite_note-111) and received mixed reviews from music critics.[[112]](#cite_note-112) It has since sold over 12 million copies worldwide,[[113]](#cite_note-113) being her highest-selling album to date.[[114]](#cite_note-114) *Teenage Dream* would go on to win the 2011 [Juno Award for International Album of the Year](/wiki/Juno_Award_for_International_Album_of_the_Year "Juno Award for International Album of the Year").[[115]](#cite_note-Juno2011-115) In October, "[Firework](/wiki/Firework_(song) "Firework (song)")" was released as the album's third single.[[116]](#cite_note-116) It became the album's third consecutive number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100 on December 8, 2010.[[117]](#cite_note-117)

"[E.T.](/wiki/E.T._(song) "E.T. (song)")" featuring [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") was released as the fourth single from *Teenage Dream* on February 16, 2011.[[118]](#cite_note-118) It topped the *Billboard* Hot 100 for five non-consecutive weeks, making *Teenage Dream* the ninth album in history to produce four number one singles on the chart.[[119]](#cite_note-119) "[Last Friday Night (T.G.I.F.)](/wiki/Last_Friday_Night_(T.G.I.F.) "Last Friday Night (T.G.I.F.)")" followed as the fifth single in June,[[120]](#cite_note-120) and Perry became the first female artist to achieve five number-one *Billboard* Hot 100 songs from one album when the single topped that chart on August 17, and the second artist after [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") with his album *[Bad](/wiki/Bad_(album) "Bad (album)")*.[[121]](#cite_note-TieMichael-121) For this record, she received an honorary [American Music Award](/wiki/American_Music_Award "American Music Award") in November 2011[[122]](#cite_note-122) and another Guinness record.[[123]](#cite_note-Guinness2013-123)

On September 7, she set a new record by becoming the first artist to spend 69 consecutive weeks in the top ten of the Hot 100.[[124]](#cite_note-adeles-someone-124) After "[The One That Got Away](/wiki/The_One_That_Got_Away_(Katy_Perry_song) "The One That Got Away (Katy Perry song)")" was released as the album's sixth single in October, *Teenage Dream* became the third album to spawn six top-five songs on the *Billboard* Hot 100 after *[Rhythm Nation 1814](/wiki/Janet_Jackson%27s_Rhythm_Nation_1814 "Janet Jackson's Rhythm Nation 1814")* by [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson"), which is the only album in the Hot 100 singles chart history to have seven songs peak within the top five positions, and *[Faith](/wiki/Faith_(George_Michael_album) "Faith (George Michael album)")* by [George Michael](/wiki/George_Michael "George Michael").[[125]](#cite_note-125)[[126]](#cite_note-126) The song peaked at number three in the U.S.[[127]](#cite_note-BiggestBillboard-127) and number two in Canada.[[128]](#cite_note-CanadaCharts-128)

[![Katy Perry looking forward and smiling](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Katy_Perry_-_Part_Of_Me_Australian_Premiere_-_June_2012_%283%29.jpg/250px-Katy_Perry_-_Part_Of_Me_Australian_Premiere_-_June_2012_%283%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Katy_Perry_-_Part_Of_Me_Australian_Premiere_-_June_2012_(3).jpg)

Perry attending the Sydney premiere for her 2012 documentary *[Katy Perry: Part of Me](/wiki/Katy_Perry:_Part_of_Me "Katy Perry: Part of Me")*, which grossed $32.7 million

On January 5, 2012, Perry was named the sixth best-selling digital artist in the United States, with sales of 37.6 million units according to [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan").[[129]](#cite_note-129) That month, she became the first artist to have four songs sell over 5 million digital units when "E.T." reached that mark along with "Firework", "California Gurls", and "Hot n Cold".[[130]](#cite_note-130) On February 13, Capitol released the lead single from *[Teenage Dream: The Complete Confection](/wiki/Teenage_Dream:_The_Complete_Confection "Teenage Dream: The Complete Confection")*, "[Part of Me](/wiki/Part_of_Me_(Katy_Perry_song) "Part of Me (Katy Perry song)")", which debuted at number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and became Perry's seventh single overall to top the chart.[[131]](#cite_note-131) *Teenage Dream: The Complete Confection* was released on March 23,[[132]](#cite_note-132) and serves as a reissue of *Teenage Dream*.[[133]](#cite_note-133) "[Wide Awake](/wiki/Wide_Awake_(Katy_Perry_song) "Wide Awake (Katy Perry song)")" was released on May 22 as the re-release's second single,[[134]](#cite_note-134) peaking at number two on the *Billboard* Hot 100[[127]](#cite_note-BiggestBillboard-127) and number one in Canada[[128]](#cite_note-CanadaCharts-128) and New Zealand.[[135]](#cite_note-135)

Perry embarked on her second tour, the [California Dreams Tour](/wiki/California_Dreams_Tour "California Dreams Tour"), in support of *Teenage Dream*[[136]](#cite_note-Hudson83-136) from February 2011 to January 2012.[[137]](#cite_note-TourBegin-137) The tour grossed $59.5 million globally[[138]](#cite_note-138) and won her the award for [Best Live Act](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Award_for_Best_Live_Act "MTV Europe Music Award for Best Live Act") at the [2011 MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/2011_MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "2011 MTV Europe Music Awards").[[139]](#cite_note-139) On September 23, 2011, she performed on the opening day of the 2011 [Rock in Rio](/wiki/Rock_in_Rio "Rock in Rio") festival along with [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John") and [Rihanna](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna").[[140]](#cite_note-140) In September 2010, Perry was scheduled to appear on the 41st-season premiere of *[Sesame Street](/wiki/Sesame_Street "Sesame Street")*. After her scene was uploaded to YouTube, viewers criticized Perry's exposed [cleavage](/wiki/Cleavage_(breasts) "Cleavage (breasts)"). Four days before the scheduled airing, [Sesame Workshop](/wiki/Sesame_Workshop "Sesame Workshop") announced that the segment would not air on television, but would still be available to watch online.[[141]](#cite_note-141) Perry subsequently mocked the controversy on *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")*, where she was a musical guest and wore an [Elmo](/wiki/Elmo "Elmo")-themed shirt showing large amounts of cleavage during one skit.[[142]](#cite_note-142)

In December 2010, Perry played [Moe Szyslak](/wiki/Moe_Szyslak "Moe Szyslak")'s girlfriend in the live-action segment from a Christmas episode of *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")* titled "[The Fight Before Christmas](/wiki/The_Fight_Before_Christmas_(The_Simpsons) "The Fight Before Christmas (The Simpsons)")".[[143]](#cite_note-Kaufman-143)[[144]](#cite_note-144) Two months later, she made a guest appearance on the *[How I Met Your Mother](/wiki/How_I_Met_Your_Mother "How I Met Your Mother")* episode "[Oh Honey](/wiki/Oh_Honey "Oh Honey")", playing a woman known as Honey.[[145]](#cite_note-145) The latter role won her the [People's Choice Award for Favorite TV Guest Star](/wiki/38th_People%27s_Choice_Awards "38th People's Choice Awards") in January 2012.[[146]](#cite_note-146) She made her film debut in the 3D family motion picture *[The Smurfs](/wiki/The_Smurfs_(film) "The Smurfs (film)")* as [Smurfette](/wiki/Smurfette "Smurfette") on July 29, 2011. The film was a financial success worldwide,[[147]](#cite_note-147) while critics gave mostly negative reviews.[[148]](#cite_note-148)

She hosted *Saturday Night Live* on December 10, 2011, with [Robyn](/wiki/Robyn "Robyn") as the episode's musical guest. Perry's work on the episode received generally positive reviews from critics, who praised her performance in the episode's [digital short](/wiki/SNL_Digital_Short "SNL Digital Short") featuring her and [Andy Samberg](/wiki/Andy_Samberg "Andy Samberg").[[149]](#cite_note-149) In March 2012, she guest starred as a prison security guard named Rikki on the *[Raising Hope](/wiki/Raising_Hope "Raising Hope")* episode "Single White Female Role Model".[[150]](#cite_note-150) On July 5, 2012, Perry's autobiographical documentary *[Katy Perry: Part of Me](/wiki/Katy_Perry:_Part_of_Me "Katy Perry: Part of Me")* was released to theaters through [Paramount Pictures](/wiki/Paramount_Pictures "Paramount Pictures").[[151]](#cite_note-151)[[152]](#cite_note-PrismaticFilm-152) The film received positive reviews[[153]](#cite_note-153) and grossed $32.7 million worldwide at the box office.[[154]](#cite_note-mojo-154)

Perry began to venture into business when she endorsed the fragrance, Purr, in November 2010. Her second endorsed fragrance, Meow!, was released in December 2011. Both perfumes were released through [Nordstrom](/wiki/Nordstrom "Nordstrom") department stores.[[155]](#cite_note-155)[[156]](#cite_note-156) [Electronic Arts](/wiki/Electronic_Arts "Electronic Arts") recruited her to promote their new expansion pack for *[The Sims 3: Showtime](/wiki/The_Sims_3:_Showtime "The Sims 3: Showtime")*,[[157]](#cite_note-157) before releasing a separate [stuff pack](/wiki/Expansion_pack "Expansion pack") featuring Perry-inspired furniture, outfits, and hairstyles, titled *[The Sims 3: Katy Perry's Sweet Treats](/wiki/The_Sims_3_Stuff_packs#Katy_Perry's_Sweet_Treats "The Sims 3 Stuff packs")*, in June 2012.[[158]](#cite_note-158) The following month, she became the spokesperson and ambassador for [Popchips](/wiki/Popchips "Popchips") and made an investment in the company.[[159]](#cite_note-Popchips2012-159) *Billboard* dubbed her as their "[Woman of the Year](/wiki/Billboard_Women_in_Music#Woman_of_the_Year_Award "Billboard Women in Music")" for 2012.[[160]](#cite_note-WomanOfTheYear-160)

She married Russell Brand on October 23, 2010, in a traditional [Hindu](/wiki/Hindu "Hindu") [ceremony](/wiki/Hindu_wedding "Hindu wedding") near the [Ranthambhore tiger sanctuary](/wiki/Ranthambore_National_Park "Ranthambore National Park") in Rajasthan.[[161]](#cite_note-Married-161) On December 30, 2011, Brand announced that they were divorcing after 14 months of marriage.[[162]](#cite_note-162) Perry later stated that conflicting career schedules and his desire to have children before she was ready led to the end of their marriage and that he never spoke to her again after sending a text message that he was divorcing her,[[163]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEPerry201201:11:49-163)[[164]](#cite_note-Divorce-164) while Brand asserted that he divorced her due to her commercial success and reluctance to engage in activism.[[165]](#cite_note-BrandDocumentary-165) She was initially distraught over their divorce and said that she contemplated suicide.[[166]](#cite_note-BillboardCoverStory-166) Since they married without a [prenuptial agreement](/wiki/Prenuptial_agreement "Prenuptial agreement"), he was eligible to claim half of the estimated $44 million she earned during their marriage, but declined.[[167]](#cite_note-167) Perry began a relationship with singer [John Mayer](/wiki/John_Mayer "John Mayer") in August 2012, the year her marriage with Brand had ended.[[168]](#cite_note-168)[[169]](#cite_note-169)

### 2013–2015: *Prism* and Super Bowl XLIX halftime show

In November 2012, Perry began work on her fourth album, *[Prism](/wiki/Prism_(Katy_Perry_album) "Prism (Katy Perry album)")*.[[170]](#cite_note-PrismMC-170)[[166]](#cite_note-BillboardCoverStory-166) She told *Billboard*, "I know exactly the record I want to make next. I know the artwork, the coloring and the tone" and "I even know what type of tour I'm doing next. I'll be very pleased if the vision I have in my head becomes a reality."[[171]](#cite_note-171) After initially telling *[L'Uomo Vogue](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)")* in June 2012 that she planned to have "darker elements" in *Prism* following the end of her marriage, the singer revealed to MTV during the [2013 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2013_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2013 MTV Video Music Awards") that she changed the album's direction after periods of self-reflection. Perry commented "I felt very prismatic", which inspired the album's name.[[172]](#cite_note-Light-172)[[173]](#cite_note-173) "[Roar](/wiki/Roar_(song) "Roar (song)")" was released as the lead single from *Prism* on August 10, 2013.[[174]](#cite_note-Roar_Release-174) It was promoted at the MTV Video Music Awards and reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[175]](#cite_note-VMA-175)[[176]](#cite_note-Roar#1-176) "[Unconditionally](/wiki/Unconditionally "Unconditionally")" followed as the second single from *Prism* on October 16, 2013,[[177]](#cite_note-UnconditionallyRelease-177) and peaked at number 14 in the United States.[[178]](#cite_note-178)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e4/Dark_horse_katy_perry.jpg/250px-Dark_horse_katy_perry.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dark_horse_katy_perry.jpg)

Katy Perry performing "Dark Horse" during the Prismatic World Tour in September 2014.

*Prism* was released on October 18, 2013,[[179]](#cite_note-179) and has sold over eight million copies worldwide.[[180]](#cite_note-180) It received favorable reviews from critics[[170]](#cite_note-PrismMC-170) and debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200 chart.[[181]](#cite_note-PrismBillboard-181) Four days later, Perry performed the songs from the album at the [iHeartRadio](/wiki/IHeartRadio "IHeartRadio") Theater in Los Angeles.[[182]](#cite_note-ReleaseParty-182) "[Dark Horse](/wiki/Dark_Horse_(Katy_Perry_song) "Dark Horse (Katy Perry song)")" with [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J") was released as the album's third single in December, and became her ninth U.S. number-one single the following month.[[183]](#cite_note-183)[[184]](#cite_note-NumberOneHorse-184) In 2014, "[Birthday](/wiki/Birthday_(Katy_Perry_song) "Birthday (Katy Perry song)")" and "[This Is How We Do](/wiki/This_Is_How_We_Do "This Is How We Do")" respectively followed as the album's fourth and fifth singles,[[185]](#cite_note-185)[[186]](#cite_note-186) and reached the top 25 on the Hot 100.[[187]](#cite_note-Hot100History-187)

Prior to ending her relationship with Mayer in 2014,[[188]](#cite_note-188) she recorded and co-wrote a duet with him titled "[Who You Love](/wiki/Who_You_Love "Who You Love")" for his album *[Paradise Valley](/wiki/Paradise_Valley_(album) "Paradise Valley (album)")*. The song was released on August 12, 2013.[[189]](#cite_note-189) Perry's third headlining tour, the [Prismatic World Tour](/wiki/Prismatic_World_Tour "Prismatic World Tour"), began in May 2014[[137]](#cite_note-TourBegin-137) and concluded in October 2015.[[190]](#cite_note-ByePrism-190) It sold almost 2 million tickets and grossed $204.3 million worldwide[[191]](#cite_note-191) and won Perry the award for "Top Package" at the 2014 [*Billboard* Touring Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Touring_Awards "Billboard Touring Awards").[[192]](#cite_note-192) She also performed at the 2015 Rock in Rio festival on September 27, 2015.[[193]](#cite_note-193)

On November 23, 2014, the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL) announced that Perry would perform at the [Super Bowl XLIX halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XLIX_halftime_show "Super Bowl XLIX halftime show") on February 1, 2015.[[194]](#cite_note-194) [Lenny Kravitz](/wiki/Lenny_Kravitz "Lenny Kravitz") and [Missy Elliott](/wiki/Missy_Elliott "Missy Elliott") served as special guests for the show.[[195]](#cite_note-195) Her performance was critically acclaimed,[[196]](#cite_note-196) and Guinness announced two days after the singer's halftime show that it garnered 118.5 million viewers in the United States, becoming the most watched and highest rated show in Super Bowl history. The viewership was higher than the game itself, which was viewed by an audience of 114.4 million.[[197]](#cite_note-Guinness2016-197)

The [International Federation of the Phonographic Industry](/wiki/International_Federation_of_the_Phonographic_Industry "International Federation of the Phonographic Industry") (IFPI) ranked her fifth on the list of Top Global Recording Artists of 2013.[[198]](#cite_note-IFPI-198) On June 26, 2014, she was declared the Top Certified Digital Artist Ever by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) for certified sales of 72 million digital singles in the United States.[[199]](#cite_note-TopCertified-199)[[200]](#cite_note-200) In May 2014, a portrait of Perry by painter [Mark Ryden](/wiki/Mark_Ryden "Mark Ryden") was featured in his exhibition "The Gay 90s", and shown at the [Kohn Gallery](/wiki/Kohn_Gallery "Kohn Gallery") in Los Angeles. Along with several other artists, she also recorded a cover version of the song "[Daisy Bell (Bicycle Built for Two)](/wiki/Daisy_Bell "Daisy Bell")" on a limited-edition concept album titled *The Gay Nineties Old Tyme Music: Daisy Bell* to accompany the exhibition.[[201]](#cite_note-MarkRyder-201) That month, a portrait of Perry by artist [Will Cotton](/wiki/Will_Cotton "Will Cotton") was included in the United States [National Portrait Gallery](/wiki/National_Portrait_Gallery_(United_States) "National Portrait Gallery (United States)").[[202]](#cite_note-202)

On June 17, 2014, Perry announced that she had founded her own record label under Capitol Records, titled Metamorphosis Music. [Ferras](/wiki/Ferras "Ferras") was the first artist to get signed to her label, and Perry served as an executive producer on [his self-titled EP](/wiki/Ferras_(EP) "Ferras (EP)"). She also recorded a duet with him on the EP, titled "Legends Never Die".[[203]](#cite_note-203) The label was later renamed [Unsub Records](/wiki/Unsub_Records "Unsub Records").[[204]](#cite_note-Unsub-204) On November 23, 2015, Perry starred in [H&M](/wiki/H%26M "H&M")'s holiday advertising campaign, for which she wrote and recorded a song titled "[Every Day Is a Holiday](/wiki/Every_Day_Is_a_Holiday "Every Day Is a Holiday")".[[205]](#cite_note-205)[[206]](#cite_note-206)

Outside of her music career, Perry reprised her role as Smurfette in *[The Smurfs 2](/wiki/The_Smurfs_2 "The Smurfs 2")*, which was released in theaters on July 31, 2013.[[207]](#cite_note-207) Like its predecessor, *The Smurfs 2* was a financial success[[208]](#cite_note-208) that was panned by critics.[[209]](#cite_note-209) In March 2014, she made a guest appearance playing herself in the episode "Blisteritos Presents Dad Academy Graduation Congraduritos Red Carpet Viewing Party" of the *[Kroll Show](/wiki/Kroll_Show "Kroll Show")*.[[210]](#cite_note-210) Killer Queen was released as her third fragrance in August 2013 through [Coty](/wiki/Coty "Coty").[[211]](#cite_note-killerqueen1-211) In January 2014, she became a guest curator of Madonna's [Art for Freedom](/wiki/Secretprojectrevolution "Secretprojectrevolution") initiative.[[212]](#cite_note-212) In March 2015, she appeared in *[Brand: A Second Coming](/wiki/Brand:_A_Second_Coming "Brand: A Second Coming")*, a documentary following her ex-husband Russell Brand's transition from comedy work to activism,[[165]](#cite_note-BrandDocumentary-165) and released a concert film titled *Katy Perry: The Prismatic World Tour* through [Epix](/wiki/Epix_(TV_network) "Epix (TV network)"), which took place during her tour of the same name.[[152]](#cite_note-PrismaticFilm-152) Perry also made a cameo appearance in the music video for Madonna's song "[Bitch I'm Madonna](/wiki/Bitch_I%27m_Madonna "Bitch I'm Madonna")" in June 2015.[[213]](#cite_note-213)

The following month, she released another fragrance with Coty, entitled Mad Potion.[[214]](#cite_note-214) In September 2015, she appeared in the documentaries *Katy Perry: Making of the Pepsi Super Bowl Halftime Show*, which followed Perry's preparation for her Super Bowl performance,[[215]](#cite_note-SuperBowl-215) and *[Jeremy Scott: The People's Designer](/wiki/Jeremy_Scott:_The_People%27s_Designer "Jeremy Scott: The People's Designer")*, which followed the life and career of designer [Jeremy Scott](/wiki/Jeremy_Scott "Jeremy Scott").[[216]](#cite_note-216) Perry released a mobile app titled *Katy Perry Pop* in December 2015 through [Glu Mobile](/wiki/Glu_Mobile "Glu Mobile") where her character helps players become famous musicians.[[217]](#cite_note-217) She described it as "the most fun, colorful world that helps guide your musical dreams".[[218]](#cite_note-218)

### 2016–2019: *Witness* and *American Idol*

Perry started writing songs for her new album in June 2016,[[219]](#cite_note-219) and recorded an anthem for [NBC Sports](/wiki/NBC_Sports "NBC Sports")' coverage of the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") titled "[Rise](/wiki/Rise_(Katy_Perry_song) "Rise (Katy Perry song)")", which was released the following month. Perry chose to release it as a standalone track rather than save it for her album "because now more than ever, there is a need for our world to unite". NBC also felt its message spoke "to the spirit of the Olympics and its athletes" for its inspirational themes.[[220]](#cite_note-220) The song reached number one in Australia[[221]](#cite_note-221) and number eleven in the United States.[[187]](#cite_note-Hot100History-187)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Katy_Perry_at_Madison_Square_Garden_%2837436531092%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Katy_Perry_at_Madison_Square_Garden_%2837436531092%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Katy_Perry_at_Madison_Square_Garden_(37436531092)_(cropped).jpg)

Perry performing during [Witness: The Tour](/wiki/Witness:_The_Tour "Witness: The Tour") in October 2017

In August 2016, Perry stated that she aspired to create material "that connects and relates and inspires"[[222]](#cite_note-222) and told [Ryan Seacrest](/wiki/Ryan_Seacrest "Ryan Seacrest") that she was "not rushing" her fifth album, adding "I'm just having a lot of fun, but experimenting and trying different producers, and different collaborators, and different styles".[[223]](#cite_note-223) On February 10, 2017, Perry released the album's lead single "[Chained to the Rhythm](/wiki/Chained_to_the_Rhythm "Chained to the Rhythm")" featuring [Skip Marley](/wiki/Skip_Marley "Skip Marley").[[224]](#cite_note-224)[[225]](#cite_note-225) It reached number one in Hungary[[226]](#cite_note-226) and number four in the United States.[[187]](#cite_note-Hot100History-187) The track was also streamed over three million times on [Spotify](/wiki/Spotify "Spotify") within 24 hours, breaking the music streaming service's record at the time for the highest first-day streaming for a single track by a female artist.[[227]](#cite_note-227) The album's second single, "[Bon Appétit](/wiki/Bon_App%C3%A9tit_(song) "Bon Appétit (song)")" with [Migos](/wiki/Migos "Migos"), was released that April.[[228]](#cite_note-228) Its third single, "[Swish Swish](/wiki/Swish_Swish "Swish Swish")", featured [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj") and followed the next month.[[229]](#cite_note-229) They respectively peaked at numbers 59 and 46 in the United States,[[187]](#cite_note-Hot100History-187) and made the top 15 in Canada.[[128]](#cite_note-CanadaCharts-128)

The album, titled *[Witness](/wiki/Witness_(Katy_Perry_album) "Witness (Katy Perry album)")*, was released on June 9, 2017, to mixed reviews,[[230]](#cite_note-230) and debuted at number one in the United States.[[231]](#cite_note-231) To accompany the album's release, Perry broadcast herself on YouTube for four days with a live-stream titled [Katy Perry Live: Witness World Wide](/wiki/Katy_Perry_Live:_Witness_World_Wide "Katy Perry Live: Witness World Wide"), concluding with a live concert on June 12.[[232]](#cite_note-232) The live-stream generated over 49 million views from 190 countries.[[233]](#cite_note-233) She also embarked on [Witness: The Tour](/wiki/Witness:_The_Tour "Witness: The Tour"), which began in September 2017 and ended in August 2018.[[234]](#cite_note-234) On June 15, 2017, [Calvin Harris](/wiki/Calvin_Harris "Calvin Harris") released a song titled "[Feels](/wiki/Feels_(song) "Feels (song)")" from his album *[Funk Wav Bounces Vol. 1](/wiki/Funk_Wav_Bounces_Vol._1 "Funk Wav Bounces Vol. 1")*, which featured Perry, [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean"), and [Pharrell Williams](/wiki/Pharrell_Williams "Pharrell Williams").[[235]](#cite_note-235) The song went on to reach number one in the United Kingdom.[[236]](#cite_note-236)

Perry subsequently recorded a cover of the *[Dear Evan Hansen](/wiki/Dear_Evan_Hansen "Dear Evan Hansen")* song "[Waving Through a Window](/wiki/Waving_Through_a_Window "Waving Through a Window")" for the deluxe edition of the [cast recording](/wiki/Cast_recording "Cast recording"), which was released on November 2, 2018.[[237]](#cite_note-Waving2-237) The show's creators [Benj Pasek](/wiki/Benj_Pasek "Benj Pasek") and [Justin Paul](/wiki/Justin_Paul "Justin Paul") had requested Perry to cover the song to promote the musical's national tour.[[238]](#cite_note-Waving1-238) Later that month, Perry released "[Cozy Little Christmas](/wiki/Cozy_Little_Christmas "Cozy Little Christmas")".[[239]](#cite_note-CozyChristmas1-239) She also recorded the song "Immortal Flame" for the game *[Final Fantasy Brave Exvius](/wiki/Final_Fantasy_Brave_Exvius "Final Fantasy Brave Exvius")*, and had a [playable character](/wiki/Playable_character "Playable character") modeled after her.[[240]](#cite_note-FinalFantasy1-240)

Outside of recording music, Perry appeared as herself in the film *[Zoolander 2](/wiki/Zoolander_2 "Zoolander 2")*, which was released in February 2016.[[241]](#cite_note-241)[[242]](#cite_note-242) In February 2017, the singer launched a shoe line titled [Katy Perry Collections](/wiki/Katy_Perry_Collections "Katy Perry Collections").[[243]](#cite_note-243) Her shoes are available on its namesake website and at retailers such as [Dillard's](/wiki/Dillard%27s "Dillard's") and [Walmart](/wiki/Walmart "Walmart").[[244]](#cite_note-244) The following August, she hosted the [2017 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2017_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2017 MTV Video Music Awards").[[245]](#cite_note-245) Perry was signed for a $25-million salary to serve as a judge on the [sixteenth season](/wiki/American_Idol_season_16 "American Idol season 16") of *American Idol*, which premiered in March 2018.[[246]](#cite_note-246)[[247]](#cite_note-247) Perry began a relationship with actor [Orlando Bloom](/wiki/Orlando_Bloom "Orlando Bloom") in early 2016 before the couple separated in February 2017.[[248]](#cite_note-248) They reconciled in 2018 and got engaged on February 14, 2019.[[249]](#cite_note-249)[[250]](#cite_note-250)

At the [61st Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/61st_Annual_Grammy_Awards "61st Annual Grammy Awards"), Perry performed "[Here You Come Again](/wiki/Here_You_Come_Again_(song) "Here You Come Again (song)")", alongside [Dolly Parton](/wiki/Dolly_Parton "Dolly Parton") and [Kacey Musgraves](/wiki/Kacey_Musgraves "Kacey Musgraves"), as part of a tribute to Parton.[[251]](#cite_note-Dolly1-251) Four days later, she released a song called "[365](/wiki/365_(Zedd_and_Katy_Perry_song) "365 (Zedd and Katy Perry song)")", with DJ [Zedd](/wiki/Zedd "Zedd").[[252]](#cite_note-252) In April, Perry was included on a remix of [Daddy Yankee](/wiki/Daddy_Yankee "Daddy Yankee")'s song "[Con Calma](/wiki/Con_Calma "Con Calma")", featuring [Snow](/wiki/Snow_(musician) "Snow (musician)").[[253]](#cite_note-253) She followed this with the singles "[Never Really Over](/wiki/Never_Really_Over "Never Really Over")" on May 31, "[Small Talk](/wiki/Small_Talk_(song) "Small Talk (song)")" on August 9, and "[Harleys in Hawaii](/wiki/Harleys_in_Hawaii "Harleys in Hawaii")" on October 16.[[254]](#cite_note-254)[[255]](#cite_note-255)[[256]](#cite_note-256) "Never Really Over" in particular received critical acclaim.[[257]](#cite_note-257) In June 2019, Perry appeared in the music video of [Taylor Swift](/wiki/Taylor_Swift "Taylor Swift")'s "[You Need to Calm Down](/wiki/You_Need_to_Calm_Down "You Need to Calm Down")".[[258]](#cite_note-258) In July, a jury in California returned a verdict following a week-long trial that Perry's song "Dark Horse" had copied [Flame](/wiki/Flame_(rapper) "Flame (rapper)")'s 2008 song "Joyful Noise" after he filed a [copyright lawsuit](/wiki/Gray_v._Perry "Gray v. Perry") alleging that it used his track's beat without permission;[[259]](#cite_note-259) the verdict was later overturned.[[260]](#cite_note-260) After the initial verdict, the jury ordered her to pay him $550,000.[[261]](#cite_note-261)

### 2020–2023: *Smile*, motherhood, and Las Vegas residency

Following the release of her single "[Never Worn White](/wiki/Never_Worn_White "Never Worn White")" in March 2020, Perry revealed in the accompanying music video that she was expecting her first child with Bloom.[[262]](#cite_note-262) "[Daisies](/wiki/Daisies_(Katy_Perry_song) "Daisies (Katy Perry song)")", the lead single from her sixth album, was released on May 15, 2020. Its second single "[Smile](/wiki/Smile_(Katy_Perry_song) "Smile (Katy Perry song)")" followed two months later.[[263]](#cite_note-263)[[264]](#cite_note-264) The album, also titled *[Smile](/wiki/Smile_(Katy_Perry_album) "Smile (Katy Perry album)")*, was released on August 28, 2020.[[265]](#cite_note-265)[[266]](#cite_note-266) Two days before its distribution, she gave birth to a daughter named Daisy Dove Bloom.[[267]](#cite_note-267) The album received mixed reviews,[[268]](#cite_note-268) and debuted at number five in the United States.[[269]](#cite_note-269) Perry further promoted the album with four compilation EPs: *Camp Katy*,[[270]](#cite_note-270) *Empowered*,[[271]](#cite_note-271) *Scorpio SZN*,[[272]](#cite_note-272) and *Cosmic Energy*.[[273]](#cite_note-273) These EPs were followed by the single "[Not the End of the World](/wiki/Not_the_End_of_the_World_(song) "Not the End of the World (song)")" in December 2020, which had a music video in which [Zooey Deschanel](/wiki/Zooey_Deschanel "Zooey Deschanel") impersonates Perry.[[274]](#cite_note-274)[[275]](#cite_note-275)[[276]](#cite_note-276) Additionally she collaborated with various artists to create two remixes of *Smile* album tracks. [Tiësto](/wiki/Ti%C3%ABsto "Tiësto") remix of "[Resilient](/wiki/Resilient_(Katy_Perry_song) "Resilient (Katy Perry song)")" featuring [Aitana](/wiki/Aitana_(singer) "Aitana (singer)") was released in November 2020, while Bruno Martini remix of "[Cry About It Later](/wiki/Cry_About_It_Later "Cry About It Later")" featuring [Luísa Sonza](/wiki/Lu%C3%ADsa_Sonza "Luísa Sonza") was released in April 2021.[[277]](#cite_note-277)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Katy_Perry_lors_de_son_concert_%22Play%22_en_2022.jpg/250px-Katy_Perry_lors_de_son_concert_%22Play%22_en_2022.jpg)](/wiki/File:Katy_Perry_lors_de_son_concert_%22Play%22_en_2022.jpg)

Perry performing on her [Play residency](/wiki/Play_(concert_residency) "Play (concert residency)") in 2022

On January 20, 2021, Perry performed "Firework" at the *[Celebrating America](/wiki/Celebrating_America "Celebrating America")* concert during the [inauguration of Joe Biden](/wiki/Inauguration_of_Joe_Biden "Inauguration of Joe Biden").[[278]](#cite_note-278) Four months later, she released a new single, "[Electric](/wiki/Electric_(Katy_Perry_song) "Electric (Katy Perry song)")", a collaboration with [Pokémon](/wiki/Pok%C3%A9mon "Pokémon") as part of its 25th anniversary.[[279]](#cite_note-279) That December, Perry followed this with "[When I'm Gone](/wiki/When_I%27m_Gone_(Alesso_and_Katy_Perry_song) "When I'm Gone (Alesso and Katy Perry song)")", a collaboration with Swedish DJ [Alesso](/wiki/Alesso "Alesso")[[280]](#cite_note-Out2021-280) that made her the third person to reach number one on Croatia's ARC 100 list across three different decades following [Lady Gaga](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") and [Coldplay](/wiki/Coldplay "Coldplay").[[281]](#cite_note-281)

Perry began hosting a [concert residency](/wiki/Concert_residency "Concert residency") named [Play](/wiki/Play_(concert_residency) "Play (concert residency)") at [Resorts World Las Vegas](/wiki/Resorts_World_Las_Vegas "Resorts World Las Vegas") on December 29, 2021.[[282]](#cite_note-282) The show's inception happened during the [COVID-19 lockdowns](/wiki/COVID-19_lockdowns "COVID-19 lockdowns") with Perry being inspired by *[Honey, I Shrunk the Kids](/wiki/Honey,_I_Shrunk_the_Kids "Honey, I Shrunk the Kids")*, *[Pee-wee's Playhouse](/wiki/Pee-wee%27s_Playhouse "Pee-wee's Playhouse")*, and *[Pee-wee's Big Adventure](/wiki/Pee-wee%27s_Big_Adventure "Pee-wee's Big Adventure")*. She described it as "larger than life" and "the kookiest, most camp show I've ever put together."[[280]](#cite_note-Out2021-280) The show has received critical acclaim[[283]](#cite_note-283) with Melinda Sheckells of [*Billboard*](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)") saying that Play's "sold-out opening night is part fantasy, part hallucination and thoroughly camp.[[284]](#cite_note-BBReview-284) In addition to a sold out opening night, the *[Santa Barbara Independent](/wiki/Santa_Barbara_Independent "Santa Barbara Independent")* reported that Perry's contract deal for the residency was worth $168 million.[[285]](#cite_note-285) It concluded on November 4, 2023,[[286]](#cite_note-286) and grossed $46.4 million.[[287]](#cite_note-PlayEarnings-287)

In September 2021, *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* paid tribute to and honored Perry in their "Power of Women" issue, where she discussed her career, motherhood, and [philanthropy](/wiki/Philanthropy "Philanthropy").[[288]](#cite_note-:0-288) As a nominee, she attended the *Variety* 2021 "Power of Women" dinner.[[289]](#cite_note-289) On her 37th birthday the next month, Perry guest hosted *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*[[290]](#cite_note-290) and starred in a holiday advertisement for [Gap Inc.](/wiki/Gap_Inc. "Gap Inc.") which featured her singing "[All You Need Is Love](/wiki/All_You_Need_Is_Love "All You Need Is Love")" by [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles"). A full version of her cover was released on streaming platforms the same day.[[291]](#cite_note-AllYouNeedIsLove-291) In January 2022, she and Morgan McLachlan established De Soi, a company which produces and sells [non-alcoholic](/wiki/Non-alcoholic_drink "Non-alcoholic drink") [apéritifs](/wiki/Ap%C3%A9ritif_and_digestif "Apéritif and digestif"). Both wanted a beverage that "would mellow the mind, minus the buzz" when creating it.[[292]](#cite_note-DeSoiBegin-292)[[293]](#cite_note-293)[[294]](#cite_note-294)

Along with [Thomas Rhett](/wiki/Thomas_Rhett "Thomas Rhett"), Perry recorded a country pop duet titled "Where We Started" for his [album of the same name](/wiki/Where_We_Started "Where We Started") that was released on April 1, 2022.[[295]](#cite_note-295) The next month, it was announced Perry would create music for the soundtrack to Jeremy Zag's animated musical film *Melody* and voice its title character.[[296]](#cite_note-296) She also became the new face for [Just Eat](/wiki/Just_Eat "Just Eat")'s, [SkipTheDishes](/wiki/SkipTheDishes "SkipTheDishes")', [Lieferando](/wiki/Lieferando "Lieferando")'s, and [Menulog](/wiki/Menulog "Menulog")'s advertisements and created a new remix of their jingle.[[297]](#cite_note-297)[[298]](#cite_note-298)[[299]](#cite_note-299)[[300]](#cite_note-300) On June 8, 2022, Perry was awarded with the Key to [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas"), the same day it was marked as Katy Perry day.[[301]](#cite_note-301)

Perry collaborated with the tech company [Apple Inc.](/wiki/Apple_Inc. "Apple Inc.") starring in advertisements for their [GarageBand](/wiki/GarageBand "GarageBand") music software where users could have "Remix Sessions" featuring her song, "Harleys in Hawaii". On the collab, she stated in August 2022: "'Harleys in Hawaii' has lived so many different lives" as well as that there was "so much opportunity to remix this song, and I can't wait to hear all the GarageBand evolutions with this Apple collab."[[302]](#cite_note-302) Perry performed at the May 2023 [Coronation Concert](/wiki/Coronation_Concert "Coronation Concert") of [Charles III](/wiki/Charles_III "Charles III") at [Windsor Castle](/wiki/Windsor_Castle "Windsor Castle").[[303]](#cite_note-303)[[304]](#cite_note-304) Four months later, she sold her music rights to Litmus Music for an estimated $225 million.[[305]](#cite_note-305)

### 2024–present: *143* and Blue Origin flight

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/KatyPerryWestminst111224_%2823_of_95%29_%2854206909700%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-KatyPerryWestminst111224_%2823_of_95%29_%2854206909700%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:KatyPerryWestminst111224_(23_of_95)_(54206909700)_(cropped).jpg)

Perry performing at [Methodist Central Hall, Westminster](/wiki/Methodist_Central_Hall,_Westminster "Methodist Central Hall, Westminster") in 2024

During an appearance on *[Jimmy Kimmel Live!](/wiki/Jimmy_Kimmel_Live! "Jimmy Kimmel Live!")* in February 2024, Perry announced her exit from *American Idol* following the conclusion of the [twenty-second season](/wiki/American_Idol_season_22 "American Idol season 22"), saying she wanted to "go out and feel that pulse to my own beat" and release new music after being "in the studio for a while". The season premiered later that month,[[306]](#cite_note-306) and concluded in May.[[307]](#cite_note-307) She released "[Woman's World](/wiki/Woman%27s_World_(Katy_Perry_song) "Woman's World (Katy Perry song)")", the first single from her seventh album, on July 11, 2024.[[308]](#cite_note-308) Two other singles preceded the record's release: "[Lifetimes](/wiki/Lifetimes_(song) "Lifetimes (song)")"[[309]](#cite_note-309)[[310]](#cite_note-310) and "[I'm His, He's Mine](/wiki/I%27m_His,_He%27s_Mine "I'm His, He's Mine")" featuring [Doechii](/wiki/Doechii "Doechii").[[311]](#cite_note-311) The album, titled *[143](/wiki/143_(Katy_Perry_album) "143 (Katy Perry album)")*, was released on September 20.[[312]](#cite_note-312) She described it as "super high energy, it's super summer, it's very high BPM" and "just full of so much joy, so much love, so much light".[[313]](#cite_note-Variety143-313) The record was panned by critics, and Perry was widely criticized for her decision to work with Dr. Luke on it after singer [Kesha](/wiki/Kesha "Kesha")'s [allegations of sexual assault](/wiki/Kesha_v._Dr._Luke "Kesha v. Dr. Luke") against him.[[314]](#cite_note-bbc24-314)[[315]](#cite_note-315)

On September 11, Perry received the [Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Video_Vanguard_Award "Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award") at the [2024 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2024_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2024 MTV Video Music Awards").[[316]](#cite_note-316) She performed during the 2024 Rock in Rio festival on the day of *143*'s release.[[317]](#cite_note-317) Eight days later, Perry headlined the pre-game entertainment at Australia's [2024 AFL Grand Final](/wiki/2024_AFL_Grand_Final "2024 AFL Grand Final").[[318]](#cite_note-318) That December, she filmed a performance at [Methodist Central Hall, Westminster](/wiki/Methodist_Central_Hall,_Westminster "Methodist Central Hall, Westminster") for a special titled *Katy Perry: Night of a Lifetime*. Produced by [Fremantle](/wiki/Fremantle_(company) "Fremantle (company)"), it premiered on [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") in the United Kingdom on December 21.[[319]](#cite_note-319) A deluxe edition of *143*, titled *1432*, was released on December 20, 2024. It contains four additional tracks, and Perry called this "an early holiday gift" for her fans.[[320]](#cite_note-320)

On April 14, 2025, she flew into space aboard [Blue Origin NS-31](/wiki/Blue_Origin_NS-31 "Blue Origin NS-31"), [Blue Origin](/wiki/Blue_Origin "Blue Origin")'s eleventh crewed flight to space under the [New Shepard](/wiki/New_Shepard "New Shepard") program, along with [Amanda Nguyen](/wiki/Amanda_Nguyen "Amanda Nguyen"), [Aisha Bowe](/wiki/Aisha_Bowe "Aisha Bowe"), [Kerianne Flynn](/wiki/Kerianne_Flynn "Kerianne Flynn"), [Gayle King](/wiki/Gayle_King "Gayle King"), and [Lauren Sánchez](/wiki/Lauren_S%C3%A1nchez "Lauren Sánchez"). It was the first all-female space flight since 1963.[[321]](#cite_note-321)[[322]](#cite_note-322)
The flight event was widely criticized for various reasons, including assertions that it was a media ploy or PR stunt for [Amazon](/wiki/Amazon_(company) "Amazon (company)").[[323]](#cite_note-323)[[324]](#cite_note-324)
Later that month, Perry embarked on her fifth concert tour, [the Lifetimes Tour](/wiki/The_Lifetimes_Tour "The Lifetimes Tour"), which ended in December. The tour grossed more than $134 million from its 91 shows.[[325]](#cite_note-325)
In July 2025, she and Bloom confirmed that they had ended their relationship the previous month.[[326]](#cite_note-326)[[327]](#cite_note-327) Perry began dating former Canadian prime minister [Justin Trudeau](/wiki/Justin_Trudeau "Justin Trudeau") later that year.[[328]](#cite_note-328)[[329]](#cite_note-people-329) On December 6, the two confirmed their relationship on Instagram.[[329]](#cite_note-people-329)[[330]](#cite_note-330) She attended the [World Economic Forum](/wiki/World_Economic_Forum "World Economic Forum")'s annual meeting in [Davos](/wiki/Davos "Davos"), Switzerland, with Trudeau in January 2026.[[331]](#cite_note-331)

On November 6, 2025, Perry released a new song titled "[Bandaids](/wiki/Bandaids_(song) "Bandaids (song)")".[[332]](#cite_note-332)

## Artistry

### Influences

[![Alanis Morissette playing guitar](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/Alanis_Morissette_5-19-2014.jpg/250px-Alanis_Morissette_5-19-2014.jpg)](/wiki/File:Alanis_Morissette_5-19-2014.jpg)

[![Freddie Mercury performing](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/Freddie_Mercury_performing_in_New_Haven%2C_CT%2C_November_1977.jpg/250px-Freddie_Mercury_performing_in_New_Haven%2C_CT%2C_November_1977.jpg)](/wiki/File:Freddie_Mercury_performing_in_New_Haven,_CT,_November_1977.jpg)

[Alanis Morissette](/wiki/Alanis_Morissette "Alanis Morissette") (left) and [Freddie Mercury](/wiki/Freddie_Mercury "Freddie Mercury") (right) both heavily influenced Perry and her music.

Perry cites her sister Angela as the woman who has had the most influence on her.[[288]](#cite_note-:0-288) During the early stages of her career, Perry's musical style gravitated towards gospel, and she aspired to be as successful as [Amy Grant](/wiki/Amy_Grant "Amy Grant").[[333]](#cite_note-333) Growing up, she could not listen to [secular music](/wiki/Secular_music "Secular music"), but at 17 a friend played her a recording of "[Killer Queen](/wiki/Killer_Queen "Killer Queen")" by [Queen](/wiki/Queen_(band) "Queen (band)"), which had a profound effect on her.[[334]](#cite_note-334)[[335]](#cite_note-335) She cites the band's frontman, [Freddie Mercury](/wiki/Freddie_Mercury "Freddie Mercury"), as her biggest influence and expressed how the "combination of his sarcastic approach to writing lyrics and his 'I don't give a fuck' attitude" inspired her music.[[336]](#cite_note-336) She paid homage to the band by naming her third fragrance Killer Queen.[[211]](#cite_note-killerqueen1-211)

Perry described [the Beach Boys](/wiki/The_Beach_Boys "The Beach Boys") and their album *[Pet Sounds](/wiki/Pet_Sounds "Pet Sounds")* as having a considerable influence on her music: "*Pet Sounds* is one of my favorite records and it influenced pretty much all of my songwriting. All of the melody choices that I make are because of *Pet Sounds*."[[337]](#cite_note-337) The singer also holds the Beatles' [eponymous album](/wiki/The_Beatles_(album) "The Beatles (album)") in high esteem, and described these two albums as "the only things I listened to for probably two years straight."[[338]](#cite_note-InterviewWiig-338)

Perry cites Alanis Morissette and her 1995 album *[Jagged Little Pill](/wiki/Jagged_Little_Pill "Jagged Little Pill")* as a significant musical inspiration. In 2012, she stated: "*Jagged Little Pill* was the most perfect female record ever made. There's a song for anyone on that record; I relate to all those songs. They're still so timeless." Additionally, Perry borrows influence from *[Flaming Red](/wiki/Flaming_Red "Flaming Red")* by [Patty Griffin](/wiki/Patty_Griffin "Patty Griffin") and *10 Cent Wings* by [Jonatha Brooke](/wiki/Jonatha_Brooke "Jonatha Brooke").[[339]](#cite_note-BillboardQA-339) Perry's autobiographical documentary *Katy Perry: Part of Me* was largely influenced by *[Madonna: Truth or Dare](/wiki/Madonna:_Truth_or_Dare "Madonna: Truth or Dare")*. She admires Madonna's ability to reinvent herself, saying "I want to evolve like Madonna".[[340]](#cite_note-340)

Perry names [Björk](/wiki/Bj%C3%B6rk "Björk") as an influence, particularly admiring her "willingness to always be taking chances".[[339]](#cite_note-BillboardQA-339) Other artists who Perry has cited as influences include [Stevie Nicks](/wiki/Stevie_Nicks "Stevie Nicks"),[[341]](#cite_note-why-341) [ABBA](/wiki/ABBA "ABBA"), [the Cardigans](/wiki/The_Cardigans "The Cardigans"),[[342]](#cite_note-342) [Whitney Houston](/wiki/Whitney_Houston "Whitney Houston"),[[343]](#cite_note-343) [Cyndi Lauper](/wiki/Cyndi_Lauper "Cyndi Lauper"),[[344]](#cite_note-344) [Ace of Base](/wiki/Ace_of_Base "Ace of Base"), [3OH!3](/wiki/3OH!3 "3OH!3"),[[345]](#cite_note-345) [CeCe Peniston](/wiki/CeCe_Peniston "CeCe Peniston"), [C+C Music Factory](/wiki/C%2BC_Music_Factory "C+C Music Factory"), [Black Box](/wiki/Black_Box_(band) "Black Box (band)"), [Crystal Waters](/wiki/Crystal_Waters "Crystal Waters"), [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey"),[[346]](#cite_note-peniston-346) [Heart](/wiki/Heart_(band) "Heart (band)"), Joni Mitchell, [Paul Simon](/wiki/Paul_Simon "Paul Simon"), [Imogen Heap](/wiki/Imogen_Heap "Imogen Heap"), [Rufus Wainwright](/wiki/Rufus_Wainwright "Rufus Wainwright"),[[347]](#cite_note-347) [Pink](/wiki/Pink_(singer) "Pink (singer)"),[[348]](#cite_note-348) and [Gwen Stefani](/wiki/Gwen_Stefani "Gwen Stefani").[[339]](#cite_note-BillboardQA-339) "Firework" was inspired by a passage in the book *[On the Road](/wiki/On_the_Road "On the Road")* by [Jack Kerouac](/wiki/Jack_Kerouac "Jack Kerouac") in which the author compares people who are full of life to fireworks that shoot across the sky and make people watch in awe.[[349]](#cite_note-349) Her second concert tour, the California Dreams Tour, was reminiscent of *[Alice's Adventures in Wonderland](/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland "Alice's Adventures in Wonderland")* and *[The Wonderful Wizard of Oz](/wiki/The_Wonderful_Wizard_of_Oz "The Wonderful Wizard of Oz")*.[[350]](#cite_note-350) She also credits the 1996 film *[The Craft](/wiki/The_Craft_(film) "The Craft (film)")* for inspiring her song "Dark Horse",[[351]](#cite_note-351) and [Eckhart Tolle](/wiki/Eckhart_Tolle "Eckhart Tolle")'s book *[The Power of Now](/wiki/The_Power_of_Now "The Power of Now")* for influencing *Prism*.[[166]](#cite_note-BillboardCoverStory-166)

### Musical style and themes

> When I am in between records, sometimes I doubt myself. I'll be like "Did I just get lucky, or did I mass-manipulate the world into thinking that seven songs were worth a number-one position?" And then I go back into the studio and I start writing, and the true essential oil of who I am comes bubbling back up and reminds me that it's always been inside of me, that nobody can take this away no matter what comment anyone makes.

—Perry on her confidence as a songwriter[[352]](#cite_note-Vogue-352)

Perry's sound has been described as [bubblegum](/wiki/Bubblegum_music "Bubblegum music") and [power pop](/wiki/Power_pop "Power pop").[[353]](#cite_note-Vulture-353)[[354]](#cite_note-All_I_Ever_Wanted-354) Before she began incorporating elements of pop, rock, and [disco](/wiki/Disco "Disco") into music, *Katy Hudson* contained gospel. Her subsequent releases, *One of the Boys* and *Teenage Dream*, involve themes of sex and love. *One of the Boys* is a [pop rock](/wiki/Pop_rock "Pop rock") record, while *Teenage Dream* features disco influences.[[355]](#cite_note-355)[[356]](#cite_note-SheffieldDream-356) Perry's fourth album, *Prism*, is significantly influenced by [dance](/wiki/Dance_music "Dance music") and pop music. Lyrically, the album addresses relationships, self-reflection, and everyday life.[[357]](#cite_note-PrismPicks-357) Her fifth studio effort *Witness* is an [electropop](/wiki/Electropop "Electropop") album that she described as a "360-degree liberation" record, with themes including political liberation, sexual liberation, and liberation from negativity.[[358]](#cite_note-WitnessLiberation-358)[[359]](#cite_note-WitnessGenre-359) Many of her songs, particularly on *Teenage Dream*, reflect on love between teenagers; *[W](/wiki/W_(magazine) "W (magazine)")* magazine described the album's sexual innuendos as "irresistible hook-laden melodies".[[34]](#cite_note-WMag-34) Self-empowerment is a common theme in Perry's music.[[360]](#cite_note-360)

Perry has described herself as a "singer-songwriter masquerading as a pop star"[[361]](#cite_note-361) and maintains that honest songwriting is very important to her. She told *[Marie Claire](/wiki/Marie_Claire "Marie Claire")*: "I feel like my secret magic trick that separates me from a lot of my peers is the bravery to be vulnerable and truthful and honest. I think you become more relatable when you're vulnerable."[[15]](#cite_note-Marie-15) Actress and comedian [Kristen Wiig](/wiki/Kristen_Wiig "Kristen Wiig") commented that "as easy, breezy, and infectious as Perry's songs can be, beneath the surface lurks a sea of mixed emotions, jumbled motives, and contradictory impulses complicated enough to fill a [Carole King](/wiki/Carole_King "Carole King") record."[[338]](#cite_note-InterviewWiig-338) According to [Greg Kot](/wiki/Greg_Kot "Greg Kot") of the *[Chicago Tribune](/wiki/Chicago_Tribune "Chicago Tribune")*, "being taken seriously may be Perry's greatest challenge yet."[[362]](#cite_note-362) In 2013, *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* labeled her "the most potent pop star of the day – her hits are relatable with just a hint of experimentation".[[363]](#cite_note-363) Randall Roberts of the *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* criticized her use of idioms and metaphors in her lyrics and for frequent "clichés".[[364]](#cite_note-LATimesReview-364) Throughout her career, Perry has also co-written songs recorded by other artists, including [Lesley Roy](/wiki/Lesley_Roy "Lesley Roy"),[[365]](#cite_note-KatyLesley-365) [Kelly Clarkson](/wiki/Kelly_Clarkson "Kelly Clarkson"),[[366]](#cite_note-AllKellyWanted-366) [Jessie James Decker](/wiki/Jessie_James_Decker "Jessie James Decker"),[[367]](#cite_note-367) [Selena Gomez & the Scene](/wiki/Selena_Gomez_%26_the_Scene "Selena Gomez & the Scene"),[[368]](#cite_note-RockGodSelena-368)[[369]](#cite_note-MoreLikeIt-369) [Britney Spears](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears"),[[370]](#cite_note-370) [Iggy Azalea](/wiki/Iggy_Azalea "Iggy Azalea"), [Rita Ora](/wiki/Rita_Ora "Rita Ora"),[[371]](#cite_note-371) [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), and [Ariana Grande](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande").[[372]](#cite_note-372)

### Voice

Perry has a [contralto](/wiki/Contralto "Contralto") [vocal range](/wiki/Voice_type "Voice type").[[373]](#cite_note-373)[[374]](#cite_note-374) Her singing has received both praise and criticism.
